info.name: IndicF5 TTS
info.description: Two APIs: the raw Python IndicF5 service, and the NestJS gateway that wraps it with auth, a job queue, and concurrency control.

variable.description: Matches an entry in gateway/.env API_KEYS
Populated by running 'Submit TTS Job' (its Tests script saves the returned jobId here)

item.name: Python TTS Service (direct)

request.description: Liveness check + whether the model is currently loaded in memory.
Eagerly loads the model into memory instead of waiting for the first /tts call. First call downloads ~1-2GB of gated model weights and can take a few minutes.
Lists available preset voices and their reference transcripts. The default voice (bn) is always used by /tts.

formdata.value: বাংলাদেশ একটি সুন্দর দেশ। এখানে অনেক নদী আছে।
formdata.description: Text to synthesize (Bengali or any supported Indic language)

request.description: Synthesizes text using the default bundled voice (bn). Takes ~15-30s on CPU. Returns audio/wav bytes directly — Postman will offer to preview/play or save the response as a file.

item.description: Calls the FastAPI service in app/ directly, on its own port. No auth, no queueing — one request blocks the whole process until synthesis finishes.

item.name: NestJS Gateway

request.description: Enqueues a synthesis job and returns immediately with a jobId (202). The Tests script above saves jobId into {{job_id}} for the next two requests. 400 on empty/oversized text, 401 on missing/invalid API key, 429 if the queue is full.
Poll this until status is 'completed' or 'failed'. Run 'Submit TTS Job' first to populate {{job_id}}. 404 if the job doesn't exist or belongs to a different API key.
Returns audio/wav bytes once the job is completed. 409 if not finished yet or the job failed, 404 for an unknown/foreign job.

item.description: Calls the gateway in gateway/ (must be running on gateway_url, with Redis up and the Python service reachable). Requires x-api-key. Flow: Submit -> Get Job Status (poll) -> Get Job Audio.
